goal: Re-verify skills audit COLLAPSE verdicts against workflow file content (not just SKILL.md body). The 2026-04-22 audit flagged 28 skills COLLAPSE based on thin SKILL.md bodies, but skills with AskUserQuestion in allowed-tools, workflow files >100 lines, or artifact contracts in <objective> tags may be real structured skills whose body is thin per the canonical skill shape (decision gad-190). Known false positives already identified: gad-plant-seed (structured interactive capture with deferred consumption), gad-auto-conventions (codebase analysis → synthesis → write), gad-write-feature-doc (needs check). Scope: for each of the 28 COLLAPSE skills, (a) read its workflow file if referenced, (b) check allowed-tools for AskUserQuestion/Task/Write/Edit, (c) check for artifact contracts declared in objective or process tags, (d) reclassify as KEEP or confirm COLLAPSE. Output: .planning/notes/2026-04-22-skills-audit-reverify.md updating each verdict with evidence. Expected ~5-10 skills move from COLLAPSE back to KEEP. Also drop any proposed CLI additions that no longer make sense after reclassification.
